key_drivers: A softer broad U.S. dollar regime continues to support non-USD FX and several commodity contracts.
Official U.S. credit conditions remain supportive, with HY spreads still relatively tight and the 2s10s curve positive.
The EIA reported a 9.0 million barrel crude inventory draw, keeping WTI biased modestly higher despite easing war-premium headlines.
Australia remains a relative macro leader thanks to hawkish RBA policy, supportive China growth, and firmer copper prices.

stale_sources: World Gold Council ETF-flow report reflects March 2026 month-end data rather than same-day live holdings
fallbacks_used: DXY, SOX, and MOVE direction were classified conservatively from same-session market pages and recent verified repository context
FedWatch stance was treated as neutral hold because the CME browser view did not expose a clean probability table in markdown
overnight_changes: FRED HY OAS latest official print: 2.84 on 2026-04-14
FRED 10Y real yield latest official print: 1.89 on 2026-04-14

imminent: Any further U.S.-Iran diplomacy or Strait of Hormuz headlines
Next key U.S. macro releases affecting rates and dollar expectations
near_term: Federal Reserve meeting later in April 2026
Additional China activity releases after the Q1 GDP print
background: Record March gold ETF outflows remain a headwind for precious metals
ECB growth projections still point to a soft euro-area macro backdrop
